evidence: Variant counts equilateral triangles of all positive side lengths (CDL25b any-size statement T_6(n)=(1/27+o(1))n^3).

evidence: Gyarfas-Lehel [GyLe95] upper bound 3/5 on the supremum of Sidon-subset proportion constants.

evidence: h(n) is the minimal m such that 2^n-1,...,m^n-1 are collectively coprime (gcd = 1).

evidence: Part (i) asks whether the density of integers with h(n)=p exists for every prime p (HasDensity in the statement).
verify_note: decl exists; HasDensity is the literal question asked

evidence: The question quantifies over all primes p and asks for the density of {n | h n = p}.

evidence: Asks whether liminf h(n) = infinity for the coprimality threshold function h.

evidence: Asks whether h(n) equals the greatest prime p with p-1 dividing n when p > n^eps, for the coprimality function h.

evidence: The statement is about the greatest prime p such that p-1 divides n.

evidence: Conjectures h(n)=3 infinitely often, i.e. 2^n-1 and 3^n-1 are coprime for infinitely many n.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research open]; h n = 3 means 2^n-1 and 3^n-1 coprime

evidence: P is the product of the first n+1 primes (a primorial) and the conjecture asks for a prime p with P+p prime.
verify_note: decl exists @[category research open]; P = product of first n+1 primes is exactly a primorial

evidence: erdosproblems.com/779 lists OEIS A005235 (Fortunate numbers); Deaconescu's conjecture concerns primes p with primorial+p prime.

evidence: The conjecture asserts existence of a prime p with p_{n+1} < p < P and P+p prime.

evidence: Asks for a set A of natural density > 1-eps (A.HasDensity delta) with the multiplicative cardinality property.

evidence: The construction takes consecutive primes p_1 < ... < p_k with sum of reciprocals just below 1.
